title: Respect prefers-reduced-motion
summary: AI-generated animations often lack prefers-reduced-motion support, ignoring vestibular accessibility needs and causing discomfort.
instruction: Wrap non-essential animations in a prefers-reduced-motion media query. Provide a motion-reduced alternative that preserves the interaction's functional outcome without visual movement.
rationale: Motion can cause vestibular discomfort, nausea, and disorientation for 15-20% of the population. WCAG requires a mechanism to pause or hide non-essential motion. A proper reduced-motion implementation protects accessibility without banning motion entirely, preserving both user comfort and animated interfaces.

violations: A hero with 5-second looping video, parallax scrolling, and animated text reveals, none wrapped in prefers-reduced-motion.
A dashboard with animated chart transitions, spinning indicators, and sliding notifications that cannot be disabled.
controls: A marketing page with CSS transitions that respect prefers-reduced-motion, replacing slide animations with crossfades.
A presentation tool with a global toggle that sets prefers-reduced-motion, disabling non-essential animations.
borderline: Essential animations like progress bars and loading spinners may remain visible in reduced-motion mode but should stop looping or reduce speed.

exceptions.description: Critical-system alerts using animation to draw attention and user-initiated video playback where the user controls playback.
examples: Progress indicator during a file upload

detector.signalDescription: Search for CSS animations, transitions, and @keyframes. Check for prefers-reduced-motion media query wrapping those animations.
limitations: JavaScript-based animation libraries (GSAP, Framer Motion) may not expose animations in static CSS.
Video autoplay is a separate concern not detectable from CSS alone.

scenarios: Micro-interactions like button hover effects that are essential to interaction feedback.
Animations that play only once on page load (not looping) with brief duration.
mitigations: Exclude micro-interactions (hover states, button presses, focus transitions under 300ms). Focus on looping, continuous, and decorative animations.

fixturePlan.description: A landing page with keyframe animations and no prefers-reduced-motion wrapping.
Same page with all animations wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) and reduced alternatives.
A page with only a button hover effect (transform: scale) which is essential feedback, no prefers-reduced-motion needed.
